Today's re-flag confirms RATIONAL conf 3 with identical underlying thesis (8-K filed 6/15, now showing in 5-day context of 24.59% move). The original catalyst was a Trump macro claim (Iran deal 'subject to finalization'), which was always binary and time-sensitive. Six days later with no follow-on headline and a slight loss, thesis is weakening rather than broken—the geopolitical narrative may have stalled or been overtaken by other news. However, UNDERDONE conf 3 calls historically hit at 67% rate in your own record, suggesting the move may still have room. ALK's fuel-hedge exposure is structurally real. Retain position at -2.56% and wait for either a catalyst update (oil stabilizes/reverses, second Trump comment, JBLU/AAL guidance shifts on fuel) or the days-horizon to elapse. At -2.56%, exit risk is limited; holding costs nothing. HOLD and monitor for re-confirmation or disconfirmation within 1-2 days.

open_positions.name: Align Technology
open_positions.thesis: no mechanism. Catalyst: Apparent positioning shift / consumer discretionary risk-on; no specific filing. Falsified by: n/a.
open_positions.latest_reasoning: Entry thesis was UNDERDONE conf 3 on apparent positioning shift / consumer-discretionary risk-on (no specific filing, weeks horizon). Position is -1.82% after 4 days. Today's re-flag on ALGN is OVERDONE conf 3 with a new specific catalyst: 8-K filed 6/22 with Items 5.02 (officer change) and 7.01 (Reg FD disclosure), paired on the same day. The re-flag argues this pairing pattern typically indicates managed succession (not surprise departure), suggesting the -4.57% one-day drop is an overreaction. However, original entry thesis was vague ('apparent positioning shift') with no specific filing citation, while new flag identifies actual company-specific news (5.02 + 7.01 combo). The juxtaposition is uncomfortable: original entry was bullish on discretionary risk-on, but actual driver is now an officer departure. Without reading the 8-K exhibits, we cannot know if the Reg FD contained guidance cuts or if the 5.02 was CFO departure with no successor. New flag's argument (5.02 + 7.01 pairing = managed succession, not crisis) is plausible but requires exhibit verification. Position is near unchanged (-1.82%) after 4 days. Thesis was weakening before the re-flag (vague setup), and the re-flag injects real company-specific uncertainty. HOLD and monitor: if the 8-K exhibits show planned retirement with clear successor and no guidance cut, ALGN recovers and thesis is redeemed; if exhibits show CFO departure mid-quarter or guidance cut, thesis is broken and we exit. Confidence low due to exhibit unknown. Default to HOLD until more clarity.

closed_positions.name: Alaska Air Group, Inc.
closed_positions.exit_reasoning: Original catalyst was Trump's Iran deal announcement → oil lower → airline fuel relief. Position is -1.64% after 2 days on a very binary, macro-dependent thesis. The flag classification was UNDERDONE (conf 3), but the catalyst has not materially progressed in 2 days. Trump said the Iran deal is 'subject to finalization' — that is not actual oil relief yet. XLE is still up, WTI has not collapsed, and the airline sector (XRU) shows no synchronized bid. The thesis is not broken, but it is clearly not playing out with urgency. Reduce to half position (sell 6.36 shares, ~50% of position) to lock in neutral price and preserve dry powder. If the Iran deal actually closes in the next 48-72 hours and oil cracks, I can re-enter. Otherwise, a binary geopolitical bet without confirmed execution is not a conviction hold. (partial)

closed_positions.name: CRISPR Therapeutics AG
closed_positions.exit_reasoning: Original thesis was UNDERDONE (sector rally + routine governance 8-K). New flag re-classifies same name as OVERDONE on the basis of the -8.97% single-day move during XBI risk-off (-3.6%), arguing the magnitude is 3x beta for a clinical-stage name. The re-flag creates a direct thesis conflict: if the 8-K items are truly annual-meeting boilerplate (5.02 board election, 5.03 charter amendments, 5.07 vote results), the original UNDERDONE thesis still applies and the new downgrade is noise. But if the items contain an unplanned executive departure or dilutive authorization, the original thesis was wrong and re-flagging is justified. After 4 days at -1.73%, no new catalyst has emerged to resolve this ambiguity. The re-flag at OVERDONE conf 3 explicitly argues the move is excessive — that is the opposite of my entry thesis. Rather than hold into horizon hoping the governance items turn out benign, I should exit and let the flag data arbitrate: if CRSP bounces hard tomorrow on confirmation that 5.02 is routine election results, I can re-enter at better levels. Current position is small (1.1% of equity) but the thesis conflict is material. Exit now, preserve capital, re-evaluate on next catalyst clarity.
